    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[26p]

    When a newborn child exhibits symptoms of intense fever, rolling eyes, clenched fists, a closed mouth and fixed gaze, salivation with heat, closed eyes, and a blue face, it is termed "frightened fetus." This occurs when the mother experiences anger or falls, leading to shock in the fetus. If the space between the eyebrows appears red and bright, it can be treated; if it is dark and dim, it cannot be treated. Administer the Supreme Sacred Life-Protecting Elixir, along with gold, silver, and light heart soup.

    When a newborn does not pass urine or stool for three to five days, this is known as "locked belly." If there is excessive heat in the womb, heat poison builds up and blocks the anus, preventing excretion. Urgently have the mother rinse her mouth with warm water, then gently massage areas on the child’s front and back, as well as the soles of the hands and feet, in total seven places, for four to five times. Next, dissolve half a drachm of light powder with a small amount of honey in warm water and administer a little frequently to promote excretion. If there is still no passage, it indicates a closure within the anus; a gold pin may be used to probe and unblock it, inserting it about two inches deep, then introduce fragrant oil mixed with honey into the hole to facilitate bowel movement. If the belly is distended and there is no milk, accompanied by groaning, after seven days, there is little chance of survival.

    If a newborn refuses to breastfeed from the beginning, it may be due to a dirty mouth that allows impurities into the stomach, causing fullness and shortness of breath, leading to an inability to suckle. There may also be vomiting or nausea, preventing milk from going down; or the child might have experienced cold while in the womb, resulting in abdominal pain, causing refusal to suckle, which leads to excessive crying. In these cases, treatment with Mu Xiang powder is recommended.[26p]

    脐风

    　　脐风者，由断脐之后，被水湿风冷之气所乘而流入心脾，遂令腹胀脐肿，四肢强直，日夜多啼，不能吮乳，甚则发为风搐。若脐青肿，撮口不开者，是为内搐，不治；爪甲黑者即死。
    　　　　　撮口

    　　撮口者，由胎气夹热，兼之风气入脐，流入心脾二经，故令舌强唇青，口撮喘急，啼声不出，不乳。若口出白沫而四肢冷者不治。撮口最为急候，一腊之内，见之尤甚。[27p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[27p]

    Umbilical Wind

    Umbilical wind occurs when, after the umbilical cord is cut, moisture from cold winds and waters enters the heart and spleen, resulting in abdominal distension and umbilical swelling, rigidity of the limbs, persistent crying day and night, and an inability to suckle. In severe cases, it may lead to convulsions. If the umbilicus is swollen and blue and the mouth cannot open, this indicates internal convulsions, which, if not treated, can be fatal; blackened nails mean death.

    Mouth Grasping

    Mouth grasping occurs due to the heat of fetal energy combined with wind entering the umbilicus, flowing into the heart and spleen channels. This causes a stiff tongue and bluish lips, with the mouth grasping for air, no sound emitted during crying, and an inability to breastfeed. If white foam comes from the mouth and the limbs are cold, treatment is ineffective. Mouth grasping is most critical, and within a month, it can become particularly severe.[27p]

    噤风

    　　噤风者，眼闭口噤，啼声渐少，吮乳不得，口吐白沫，大小便皆不通。亦由胎中受热，热毒流入心脾，故形见于喉舌间也。　　此上三证，其名虽异，受病之源则一。初生七日，得此证者，多不可治。若不急救，坐以待死，良可惜焉！　　但见小儿喷嚏，多啼身热，不乳，急看小儿上腭有小泡如粟米大，或以指甲，或以穵耳轻轻刮去，以绢缠手指蘸温水，拭净其血，勿令下咽，便安妥，不必服药，诚良法也。　　如儿牙关紧急，已成撮口惊搐者，先用撮风散以开其关，次用控痰散以吐其痰，后用益脾散以和其胃，保命丹袪惊即愈。若手足挛拳，口噤不开者，不治。　　亦有热在胸膛，伸缩无时，呢呢作声，努胀其气。以致脐突浮肿，此非断脐使之然也，但散其热自愈，加减龙胆汤主之。　　亦有肚胀青筋，吊肠卵疝，内气引痛而撮口者，皆肠胃郁结不通致之。治法贵乎疏利，紫霜丸量而与之，一粒金丹尤妙。[28p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[28p]

    Wind Seizure

    Wind seizure is identified by closed eyes and sealed lips, a gradually diminishing cry, inability to suckle, foaming at the mouth, and obstruction of urination and defecation. This condition results from heat in the womb, with hot poison flowing into the heart and spleen, hence manifesting in the throat and tongue.

    The above three symptoms, though differing in name, share the same origin of disease. If a newborn exhibits these symptoms within the first seven days, it is often untreatable. If urgent rescue is not performed and one waits idly for death, it would indeed be a pity!

    If a child sneezes frequently, cries a lot, runs a fever, and refuses to suckle, one should urgently check for small bubbles on the child’s hard palate, approximately the size of millet. Gently scrape these with a fingernail or a soft cotton swab, blot them with a hand wrapped in fine cloth soaked in warm water, cleaning away the blood without allowing it to be swallowed; this would bring security and there is no need for medication, truly an excellent method.

    If the child has a tightly clenched jaw resulting in convulsions, one should first use a wind-dispelling powder to open the jaw, then employ an expectorant to expel phlegm, followed by a spleen tonifying powder to harmonize the stomach. The life-preserving pill can dispel fright and thus cure the issue. However, if the hands and feet are clenched and the mouth cannot be opened, it is beyond treatment.

    There are also cases of heat in the chest, which expands and contracts unpredictably, causing a soft, lingering sound, with bloating around the navel. This is not due to the umbilical cord being cut incorrectly, but simply requires the dispersal of heat for self-healing, and adjustments can be made with Longdan Decoction as the primary treatment.

    In addition, if the abdomen is bloated with blue veins, indicating a hanging intestine or hernia, with internal gas causing pain and a clenched mouth, this results from obstruction in the intestines and stomach. The treatment focuses on relieving and unblocking; a measured dose of Zishuang Pills will aid, and a single Golden Pill is particularly effective.[28p]

    西江月

    　　胎黄状如金色，身热大便难通，小便黄赤色蒙胧，少乳时时舌弄。此证传来母毒，脾胃湿热相攻，凉经凉血解重重，保养胎元兼用。　　外有胎肥胎瘦，此为禀赋虚羸，父精母血必多亏，儿子不充元气。此个甚难调理，愚夫不晓支持，一朝有病至倾危，却把命来找对。　　生下时时吐奶，不思乳食昏沉，此为秽物下咽门，拭洗未能洁净。会厌中间阻隔，太仓上口留凝，豁痰顺气药通神，炮制生姜作引。　　生下忽然腹胀，脐中血水淋漓，断脐将息失其宜，客水邪风侵入。[29p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[29p]

    Western River Moon

    The胎黄 (tai huang) appears golden, the body is hot and bowel movements are difficult, urine is yellow and unclear, and there is little milk with the tongue often moving. This condition is caused by maternal toxins, with damp heat attacking the spleen and stomach; cooling the channels and blood to resolve the severity, nourishing the fetal essence as well.

    Externally, there are both overweight and underweight fetuses; this indicates a deficiency in constitution, with insufficient paternal essence and maternal blood, resulting in the child lacking vital energy. This is quite difficult to regulate, and foolish individuals do not understand how to support it. When illness arises suddenly, it can lead to dire consequences, yet one seeks a remedy for life.

    Upon birth, there is often vomiting of milk, a lack of interest in milk and food, and lethargy; this indicates debris obstructing the swallowing passage, which has not been thoroughly cleansed. There is an obstruction in the epiglottis, and stasis left in the upper stomach; one should utilize phlegm-dissipating and qi-regulating remedies to clear the spirit, using prepared ginger as a guide.

    Suddenly after birth, there may be abdominal distension, with blood and fluid leaking from the umbilical cord; if the cord is cut and the rest is not appropriately handled, external water and邪风 (zhai feng) can invade.[29p]

    外用灰矾粘贴，速令干较为奇，若还撮口哭声稀，纵有神丹莫治。　　小儿生下数日，睡中啼哭多惊，此因母气失和平，常因七情为病。以致胞胎气逆，痰涎流入脾心，治须顺气更精神，镇坠痰涎始定。　　胎热遍身如火，发斑丹毒风疮，神昏目痛又惊章，大小便难哭嚷。此是母贪煎炒，温经暖药乖方，急须解毒令清凉，甘草黄连为上。　　胎寒生来吐泻，大便滑溜多清，腹中疼痛哭声频，面色青白不定。平日母多生冷，寒邢传入胎经，治宜丸散用甘温，可保婴儿性命。[30p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[30p]

    For external application, use alum paste, and it dries quickly, which is rather strange; if the child still cries with a weak voice, even the divine elixir cannot cure it.

    When a baby is born, for several days, it often cries in its sleep, startled. This is due to the mother’s qi being unbalanced, often caused by emotional disturbances leading to illness. This results in fetal qi being reversed, with phlegm entering the spleen and heart. Treatment must harmonize the qi and rejuvenate the spirit, calming the phlegm to stabilize the condition.

    If the fetus is overheated and the body feels like it’s on fire, with rashes, toxins, and wind sores, accompanied by confusion, eye pain, and jolted shock, along with difficulty in urination and bowel movements, this is because the mother has been greedy with frying and warming medicines, using inappropriate methods. It’s urgent to detoxify and cool down, with licorice and huanglian being the best options.

    If the fetus is cold at birth, resulting in vomiting and diarrhea, with stools being slippery and clear, accompanied by frequent crying due to abdominal pain, and a complexion that is pale and fluctuates, this is because the mother often consumes cold foods, transmitting coldness into the fetal channels. Treatment should be using warm pills or powders, which can safeguard the infant's life.[30p]

    小儿初生病证，许多名状难同，胎惊撮口共脐风，寒热瘦肥黄肿。呕吐昏昏不乳，脐间血水溶溶，未从满月病来凶，好似风中烛弄。　　最是脐风可畏，三朝八日为殃，初然喷嚏似风伤，啼哭时时吵嚷。急看小儿上腭，刮破白泡中央，展揩恶血细端详，莫使下喉为上！　　若是不知此法，致令泡落儿吞，忽然腹胀满膨膨，脐肿青筋散乱。撮口昏昏不乳，目瞪又紧牙关，啼声不出命归天，劝取衣棺急办。　　小儿为何难治？古今号曰哑科，脉无可诊待如何，口不能言病作。[31p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[31p]

    Infants, upon birth, may present with many challenging symptoms. They may experience disturbances due to fright from the womb, a combination of umbilical wind, with manifestations of cold and heat, fluctuations in weight, and yellowish swelling. Vomiting leads to a state of confusion with refusal to nurse; there may be blood and water seeping from the navel, and ailments from before the full moon may be ominous, resembling a candle flickering in the wind.

    The most feared condition is umbilical wind, leading to calamity within three days or eight days. Initially, there may be sneezing similar to wind injury, and cries that are loud and incessant. One must urgently examine the infant's palate, scraping the white blister at its center, and carefully observe any foul blood, ensuring it does not affect the throat!

    If one is unaware of this method, the blister may burst and the child might swallow it, leading to sudden abdominal bloating and a swollen navel with dilated veins. The child becomes lethargic, refusing to nurse, eyes open wide, jaws tightly clenched, unable to cry, and life may be lost. It is advised to prepare clothing and a coffin urgently.

    Why is it so difficult to treat infants? From ancient times to the present, this has been termed the silent syndrome; if the pulse cannot be diagnosed, what can be done if the mouth cannot articulate the illness?[31p]

    父母时时惊怕，医人试验诚多，从容审证用方药，有甚难为捉摸。　　要识小儿治法，方为业擅专门，半周一岁病何因？胎毒单单现证。自后饮食渐广，肥甘之变须明，此时脾胃病多寻，消食养脾法定。　　小儿纯阳之体，阴阳不可偏伤，常带三分饥与凉，此个孩儿易养。大抵脾常不足，有余肝气须防，不寒不热药为良，切忌妄行孟浪！　　胎病要行解毒，无如甘草黄连，若加脾弱病相缠，参朮陈皮有验。巴豆牵牛丁桂，砒硫白汞青铅，俱伤正气损真元，悞了孩儿命短。[32p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[32p]

    Parents are often fearful; doctors have many experiments to try. It requires careful examination and verification of remedies, as there are many difficulties in understanding them.

    To know the methods for treating children, one must be specialized in this field. What are the causes of a child's illness at half a year or one year? The signs of fetal toxins are often evident. Later, as the diet expands, one must be careful of fatty and sweet foods, as this period often sees many stomach and spleen ailments, necessitating methods to digest food and nourish the spleen.

    Children are pure yang beings; one must not excessively harm yin or yang. They often maintain a slight hunger and coolness, making them easier to care for. Generally, the spleen is often insufficient, while liver qi may be excessive; careful balance is required, and neutral medicines are best—reckless actions should be avoided!

    For fetal diseases, detoxification is key, such as with licorice and yellow bitter herb. If weakness of the spleen is entangled with illness, ginseng, atractylodes, and aged tangerine peel have proven effective. Medicines like croton seeds, morning glory, cinnamon, arsenic, sulfur, white mercury, and lead can all harm vital energy and damage true essence, risking the child's life and leading to a shortened lifespan.[32p]

    变蒸

    　　此小儿正病者，盖变者变易也，每变毕即性情有异于前。何者？生长脏腑之意也。蒸者，蒸蒸然热也。万物生于春，长于夏者，以阳主生长也。于人亦然。所以变蒸足始乃成人，血气充实骨肉坚牢也。小儿此证譬如蚕之有眠，龙之脱骨，虎之转爪，而变化同也。故每三十二日有一变蒸，至五百七十六日，大小变蒸足矣。轻则发热微汗，其状似惊；重则壮热脉乱而数，或汗，或烦啼躁渴。轻者五日解，重者八日解。其候与伤寒相似。亦有变蒸之余感寒邪者，但变蒸则耳冷，上唇发泡，状如浊珠；若寒邪搏之，则寒热交争，腹中作痛，而啼哭之声日夜不绝。治此之法，轻者不须服药，重者以平和饮子微表之。热甚便秘，以紫霜丸微利之；若吐泻或不乳而多啼者，以调气散治之。　　凡小儿当变蒸之时，不热不惊，别无他证者，是为暗变，此受胎气壮盛故也。[33p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[33p]

    Transformation and Steaming

    This child is indeed ill; "transformation" refers to change. Each transformation alters the disposition from what it was before. Why is this? It is due to the growth and development of the internal organs. "Steaming" signifies a gradual increase in heat. All things are born in spring and thrive in summer because Yang governs growth. This is also true for humans. Thus, the transformation and steaming must occur for one to grow into adulthood, with blood and qi becoming full, and bones and flesh strong and solid. This condition in children is akin to the silkworm entering dormancy, the dragon shedding its bones, or the tiger retracting its claws; all undergo similar changes. Therefore, every thirty-two days there is a transformation and steaming, and this continues for five hundred seventy-six days; both minor and major transformations and steamings are enough.

    If mild, there may be slight fever and perspiration, resembling a frightful state; if severe, there is intense heat, a chaotic and rapid pulse, possibly accompanied by sweating, irritability, crying, and thirst. In mild cases, symptoms resolve in five days; in severe cases, they resolve in eight days. The symptoms are similar to those of a cold. There are also instances where the residual effects of transformation and steaming encounter cold pathogens; in such cases, the ears feel cold, and bubbles form on the upper lip, resembling murky pearls. If cold pathogens collide with this, there will be a conflict of cold and heat leading to abdominal pain, and incessant cries day and night.

    For treatment, mild cases require no medication, while severe cases should take a calming herbal drink to slightly alleviate the surface. If there is significant heat leading to constipation, one can use a mildly laxative formula. If there are symptoms of vomiting, diarrhea, or excessive crying without nursing, one should use a qi-regulating powder for treatment. Whenever children are undergoing transformation and steaming, if they are not hot, not startled, and show no other symptoms, this is considered a "hidden transformation," due to the robust prenatal qi.[33p]

    西江月

    　　小儿变蒸何以？三十二日为期，精神改变异常时，发热蒸蒸昏睡。遇过风寒外感，或兼乳食伤脾，留连苦楚莫差池，好把汤丸调治。　　大抵六十四日，初生肾与膀胱，再生心火与小肠，肝胆第三长养。肺与大肠居四，脾胃五次猜详，三焦包络不同乡，只为有名无状。　　变蒸发热，此小儿正病，不须服药，当于类下求之，不可错误！　　小儿变生智慧，自然发热如蒸，昏昏不乳欠醒醒，恰似蚕眠相应。医者不须妄治，父母何必忧惊，三日之后自和平，莫怕别生形证。　　若遇风寒外感，惺惺散子堪行，内伤乳食不安宁，保和养脾兼进。咳嗽参苏饮子，吐泄理中最灵，惊来搐掣用泻青，导赤亦宜选用。[34p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[34p]

    West River Moon

    How does a child undergo transformation? The period lasts thirty-two days, during which the spirit changes abnormally, leading to fever and a drowsy state. If there’s a response to wind and cold from outside, or if food and milk harm the spleen, lingering pain should not be taken lightly; it’s best to use soup balls for treatment.

    Generally, after sixty-four days, the newborn's kidneys and bladder mature, followed by the revitalization of heart fire and small intestine, and then the growth of liver and gallbladder. The lungs and large intestine rank fourth, while the spleen and stomach require careful consideration fifth; the three heating areas are distinct but misguided by mere names.

    When undergoing transformation and fever, this is the child’s real ailment; there is no need for medication, but one must seek answers accordingly to avoid mistakes!

    When a child’s transformation brings wisdom, fever arises naturally, producing a sleepy state with a lack of desire for milk, just like a silkworm in its slumber. Doctors need not treat blindly, nor should parents worry excessively; after three days, peace will return naturally, so there’s no need to fear other symptoms.

    If there’s an encounter with wind and cold, it’s reasonable to use Xiao Feng San. For internal discomfort from food and milk, Baohua should be administered to nourish the spleen. For cough, Shen Su Yin is effective, and for vomiting and diarrhea, Li Zhong is most effective. In cases of fear leading to cramps, use Xie Qing, and Daodi will also be suitable.[34p]

    奇效良方 【明　方贤】

    　　　　　辨小儿生下三月已前中病不治
    　　风噤　猢狲噤　脐风　撮口　咳嗽　伏声　木舌　重腭　解颅　项强　胸高
    　　　　　胎热

    　　凡小儿胎热者，但看初生下肌肤红白，二月已后，遍身黄肿，眼闭不开，作呻吟声。此因胎内有热，或因母服热药所致，亦谓之血疸。治之以辰砂金箔散、牛黄膏乳上吮之，兼以黄蘗皮煎汤浴之，此良法也。
    　　　　　变蒸

    　　观诸变蒸热作惊，须见日角左边眉间脉红是也。大凡初蒸见一条，长一二分。在眉上者轻，自日角垂至眉上者重。变蒸发热，见二条红者，两三次蒸。热在内不解，脉红带叉，因惊而蒸脉青。变蒸多次，青在左太阳，因伤风而蒸。自顖门青至眉之上，因惊而蒸。三处皆青，三证皆见。若及三十二齿者，变蒸足也。[35p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[35p]

    Remarkable Efficacy of Good Remedies
    [Ming Dynasty - Fang Xian]

    Differentiating Childhood Illnesses not Treated Before Three Months
    Wind Shen, Monkey Shen, Umbilical Wind, Mouth Clenching, Cough, Hidden Voice, Wood Tongue, Heavy Jaw, Unclenching Skull, Stiff Neck, High Chest, Fetal Heat.

    For infants with fetal heat, one should observe if their skin appears red or white immediately after birth. After two months, if their body becomes yellow and swollen, and their eyes cannot open, accompanied by groaning sounds, this is due to internal heat or possibly a result of the mother taking hot medicines, also known as blood jaundice. To treat this, one should use a powder made from mercurial cinnabar and gold leaf, applied with bovine bile ointment, and additionally bathe with decocted yellow bark; this is an excellent method.

    Transformative Fever

    Observing the manifestations of transformative fever, if accompanied by fright, one must check for a red pulse between the left eyebrow of the sun's angle. Generally, in early stages, there is one red line that is one or two centimeters long. If located above the eyebrow, it is mild; if it stretches from the sun's angle to above the eyebrow, it is severe. When the transformative fever causes heat, two red lines indicate multiple episodes of fever. If the heat within remains unresolved, the pulse will be red with branches, turning blue due to fright. Recurring transformative fever often leads to a blue condition in the left temple, resulting from wind invasion. If blue lines extend from the temples to above the eyebrows, this is due to fright. All three areas show blue, all symptoms present. If involving thirty-two teeth, the transformative fever is complete.[35p]

    片玉心书 【明　万全】

    　　　　　初生诸疾胎疾

    　　初生三五日，大便血尝来。黑色为胎粪，鲜红实可哀。初生便呕吐，胞浆蓄胃中。物尽吐自止，不止便为凶。小儿初生十日内，少乳多啼常喷嚏。急看喉中有珠泡。手法刮去免忧虑。　　怕看撮脐风，撮脐粪少通。急用解毒散，便下得从容。便闭肚膨胀，口紧咬唇青。时时手足掣，脐风枉用心。腹胀不便名锁肚，口紧不乳是噤风。目视叫哭盘肠钓，天钓身仰似角弓。初生芽儿有此病，父母欢喜一场空。　　变蒸休用药，三日自然安。外感惺惺散，伤食保和丸。胎热甘草黄连汤，胎寒去连加桂方。胎黄甘草加茵陈，胎惊辰砂一味良。胎瘦胎肥浴体法，胎怯五软用地黄。此是家传真口诀，儿孙记诵莫遗忘！　　肝色多青心赤红，脾黄肺白肾黑同。黄白疳虚黑中恶，赤红是热青惊风。　　语迟者，由儿在胎之时，母受惊邪之气乘心，儿感母气，心神不定，不能荣舌，故尔语迟。菖蒲丸主之。[36p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[36p]

    Heartfelt Writings on the Symptoms of Infants [Ming, Wanquan]

    In the initial days after birth, the infant may exhibit various ailments.

    In the first three to five days, blood in the stool may occur. Black signifies meconium, while bright red is truly concerning. If the newborn vomits soon after birth, it is due to amniotic fluid accumulating in the stomach. Once everything is expelled, it should cease; if not, it is a bad omen.

    Within the first ten days, infants may have insufficient milk and frequently cry, often sneezing. It is urgent to check for blisters in the throat. Using manual techniques to scrape them away can alleviate worries.

    Beware of umbilical wind, and if there are few bowel movements, act quickly with a detoxifying powder to facilitate a smooth passage. If there is constipation with a bloated belly and a tightly closed mouth causing pale lips, the baby may flail their arms and legs, and treating umbilical wind may prove futile. Abdominal distension without relief is referred to as a locked belly; if the mouth is tightly closed and the infant cannot nurse, it signals a wind condition. If the baby cries while looking around, it may indicate internal distress; when distressed, the body may arch like a drawn bow.

    If a newborn presents these conditions, parents may feel joy but ultimately find it unfulfilled. Discontinue medication and allow three days for natural stabilization. For external symptoms, use a soothing powder; for dietary disturbances, employ Baohe Wan. For internal heat, use a mixture of licorice and coptis; for cold conditions, add cinnamon. For jaundice, combine licorice and artemisia; for fright, one effective remedy is cinnabar. For thin or plump infants, bathing them can be beneficial, and for timidity or weakness, use Rehmannia.

    This is a family-proven formula; may children and grandchildren memorize it without forgetting!

    The liver may appear greenish, the heart red, the spleen yellow, the lungs white, and the kidneys black. Yellow and white spots indicate deficiency, while black signifies malevolence; red and green denote heat and fright.

    If speech is delayed, it may result from the mother experiencing fear or negative energy during pregnancy, affecting the child. This causes instability in the mind and prevents the child from developing their speaking ability. The use of sweet flag pills is recommended for this.[36p]

    受胎，母即有惊邪二气乘心，舌木加菖蒲、茯神、参、远志、麦冬、当归、乳香、砂，蜜丸粟大，吞二十，薄荷汤下，语迟瘥。　　夜啼之证有四：惊啼，热烦啼，腹痛啼，神不安啼。　　惊啼者，邪热乘于心也，当安心，以导赤散加灯心退心热，以安神丸定心，效。　　热烦啼者，其哭无泪，见灯则喜而止，以导赤散加麦冬、栀子仁治之。　　腹痛啼者，脏冷而痛也，面青而无光，以温中药调理中丸、益黄散治之。　　神不安啼者，睡中忽觉自哭，以安神丸灯心烧灰，调汤吞服。[37p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[37p]

    To conceive, the mother experiences two evil energies disturbing her heart: the spirit of fright and heat. Add ingredients like sweet flag, Poria, ginseng, Polygala, Ophiopogon, Angelica, frankincense, and sand, mix into pills the size of millet, take twenty, and swallow with peppermint tea to ensure healing.

    There are four types of night crying: frightened crying, heat-related crying, abdominal pain crying, and restless crying.

    For frightened crying, it is due to evil heat disturbing the heart. Calm the heart with a mixture of Red Scattered Powder and lampwick to dispel the heart heat, and use calming pills to stabilize the heart for effectiveness.

    For heat-related crying, the child cries without tears and stops upon seeing a light, treat with Red Scattered Powder mixed with Ophiopogon and Gardenia seeds.

    For abdominal pain crying, the abdomen is cold and painful, the face is pale and lacks luster; remedy this with warming herbs and adjust with Middle Regulating Pills and Beneficial Yellow Powder.

    For restless crying, the child suddenly wakes from sleep crying. Use calming pills made from lampwick ash mixed with tea to swallow.[37p]

    又有拂其性而拗哭者，要审明白，不可妄投汤丸。　　袓传治夜啼，以至圣保命丹，灯心灰调汤下，甚效。　　小儿夜啼四证，忤惊肚痛心烦，如逢拗哭忤家言，睡中忽啼惊见。肚痛手足厥冷，腰曲口气冰寒，心热烦躁不安眠，其证面赤腹暖。　　既辨夜啼证候，其间治法须明，分明传授与人间，只得心诚求遍。忤惊安神丸子，理中专治脾痛，凉惊锭子治心烦，总用灯花妙散。　　啼哭直视热在肝，泻青一服即时安。啼哭面赤热在心，导赤麦冬效如神。　　面赤四逆腹中痛，益黄散用姜汤送。夜夜见灯多拗哭，父母娇爱多不足。[38p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[38p]

    There are also those who cry out of their nature and are difficult to console; one must examine and understand clearly, and not recklessly use treatments. Ancestral teachings for treating night crying include the supreme life-preserving elixir, which is mixed with lampwick ash and administered as a soup with great effect.

    For a child’s night crying, there are four signs: being startled, having stomach pain, feeling irritable, and when the inappropriate crying corresponds with family words, suddenly crying out in sleep. Symptoms include stomach pain, cold hands and feet, a bending waist, and breath that feels icy, with a hot and restless heart unable to sleep; in such cases, the face is red and the abdomen warm.

    Once the signs of night crying are discerned, the treatment methods must be clear and explicitly passed on to others; sincerity in seeking help is essential. For calming one who is startled, there are calming pills, while treatments specifically targeting stomach pain include cooling pills for irritability, all using a brilliant powder made from lamp flowers.

    Crying directly indicates heat in the liver; a dose of xieqing will bring immediate calm. If the crying indicates a red face with heat in the heart, guiding with red dates and coptis has miraculous effects.

    For a red face and cold extremities, abdominal pain can be alleviated with beneficial yellow powder mixed with ginger soup. If the child cries excessively at night, it is often due to excessive pampering from parents.[38p]

    幼科发挥 【明　万全】

    　　　　　胎疾

    　　小儿初生至周岁者，皆为胎疾。
    　　气，阳也；血，阴也。人之有生，受气于父，阳之变也；成形于母，阴之合也。阴阳变合而成其身。身之中，形脏四：头面一也，耳目口鼻二也，手足三也，皮肉筋骨四也；神脏五：心藏神，肝藏魂，脾藏意，肺藏魄，肾藏志是也。凡五脏者，皆父母一体而分者也。形拘于一偏而不能相通者，阴之静也；神随感而动者，阳之动也。儿之初生，只是一块血肉耳，虽有形而无所用，虽有五脏而无其神，犹空脏也。至于变蒸之后，皮肉筋骨以渐而坚，声色臭味以渐而加，志意智慧以渐而发，知觉运动而始成童，此天地生物之心，至诚不息。　　有因父母禀受所生者，胎弱胎毒是也。胎弱者，禀受于气之不足也。子于父母，一体而分。如受肺之气为皮毛，肺气不足，则皮脆薄怯寒。[39p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[39p]

    Fetal Diseases

    All infants from birth to one year old are considered to have fetal diseases.

    Qi is yang; blood is yin. The existence of a person is due to receiving qi from the father, which is the transformation of yang; and forming in the mother, which is the union of yin. Yin and yang combine to create the body. Within the body, there are four physical organs: the head and face as one, the ears, eyes, mouth, and nose as two, the hands and feet as three, and the skin, flesh, tendons, and bones as four; and five spiritual organs: the heart houses the spirit, the liver houses the soul, the spleen houses intention, the lungs house the corporeal soul, and the kidneys house will. All five organs are derived as divisions of a single entity from the parents. The form is limited and cannot connect, which reflects the stillness of yin; the spirit moves in response and is thus the movement of yang. An infant at birth is merely a mass of flesh and blood; it has form but lacks function, and though it possesses five organs, it has no spirit, akin to being an empty vessel. As time progresses, the skin, flesh, tendons, and bones gradually strengthen, the senses of sound, color, smell, and taste gradually develop, and intentions, thoughts, and wisdom gradually emerge, culminating in the child's ability to perceive and move, thus becoming a fully formed being. This is the essence of life as born from heaven and earth, genuine and unceasing.

    There are also those affected by the conditions inherited from their parents, namely weak and toxic fetuses. A weak fetus is derived from insufficient qi. A child is a division of its parents, and if it is affected by the lungs' qi, it will manifest as fragile and cold skin, due to insufficient lung qi.[39p]

    毛发不生；受心之气为血脉，心气不足，则血不华色，面无光彩；受脾之气为肉，脾气不足，则肌肉不生，手足如削；受肝之气为筋，肝气不足，则筋不束骨，机关不利；受肾之气为骨，肾气不足则骨软。此胎禀之病，当随其脏气求之。肝肾心气不足，宜六味地黄丸主之；脾肺不足者，宜参苓白朮丸主之。子之羸弱，皆父母精血之弱也。所谓父强母弱生女必羸，父弱母强生男必弱者是也。[40p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[40p]

    Hair does not grow; the heart's energy influences the blood vessels. If the heart's energy is insufficient, the blood lacks vitality and the face lacks luster. The spleen's energy relates to the flesh; if the spleen's energy is insufficient, the muscles do not develop, and the hands and feet appear emaciated. The liver's energy pertains to the tendons; if the liver's energy is insufficient, the tendons do not support the bones, and the functions of the body are impaired. The kidneys' energy is linked to the bones; if the kidneys' energy is insufficient, the bones are soft. This pertains to congenital conditions affecting the organs' energy. For insufficiency in the liver, kidneys, and heart, one should use the Six Flavor Rehmannia Pill; for deficiency in the spleen and lungs, the Ginseng, Poria, and White Atractylodes Pill is appropriate. The weakness of a child reflects the weakness of the parents' essence and blood. It is said that if a strong father and weak mother conceive a daughter, she will surely be weak; conversely, a weak father and strong mother will produce a son who is also weak.[40p]

    故儿有头破颅解，神慢气少，项软头倾，手足痿弱，齿生不齐，发生不黑，行走坐立，要人扶掖，皆胎禀不足也。并宜六味地黄丸主之。　　或问胎禀不足之证，得于父母，有生之初，如何医得？予曰：诸器破损者，尚可补之，岂谓胎弱者不可补之乎？贵得其要也。夫男女之生，受气于父，成形于母。故父母强者，生子亦强；父母弱者，生子亦弱。所以肥瘦长短大小姸媸，皆肖父母也。儿受父母之精血以生，凡五脏不足者，古人生地黄丸主之。或曰：五脏不足而专补肾，何也？曰：太极切分，天一生水。精血妙合，先生两肾，肾者五脏之根本也。经云：植木者必培其根。此之谓也。
    　　　　　胎惊

    　　初生月内非脐风证发搐者，此胎惊也。宜至圣保命丹，金银磨水送下；或用全蝎一枚，薄荷叶包炙为末，朱砂末三分和匀，猪乳调五粒许。如常发者，名胎癎，不可治也。[41p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[41p]

    If a child has a head injury, is slow in response, has weak vitality, a soft neck, a tilted head, weak limbs, uneven teeth, a non-black complexion, struggles with walking or sitting, and requires support from others, this indicates insufficient prenatal endowment. It is advisable to treat it with Liu Wei Di Huang Wan.

    If asked about the signs of insufficient prenatal endowment that arise from parents since birth, how can it be treated? I would say: even damaged vessels can be repaired; how can it be said that a weak prenatal state cannot be supplemented? What matters is to grasp the essentials. The birth of a child, whether male or female, receives energy from the father and forms in the mother. Therefore, strong parents give rise to strong children; weak parents give rise to weak children. Thus, traits of body type, size, and beauty reflect those of the parents. The child receives essence and blood from the parents; for any insufficiency of the five organs, the ancient remedy Liu Wei Di Huang Wan is indicated.

    Some may ask: if the five organs are insufficient, why focus on supplementing the kidneys? The answer is: the Taiji divides, from which water is born. Essence and blood harmonize to create the two kidneys; the kidneys are the foundation of the five organs. The classics say: those who plant trees must nourish their roots. This is what is meant.

    胎惊 (Prenatal Fright)

    If, within the first month after birth, a child shows symptoms of convulsions not due to umbilical wind, this is known as prenatal fright. It is advisable to use the Shengbao Ming Dan, ground gold and silver mixed with water to administer. Alternatively, one may use a whole scorpion wrapped in mint leaves, roasted and ground, mixed with three parts of vermilion powder and adjusted with about five grains of pig milk. If it occurs regularly, it is termed prenatal epilepsy, which cannot be treated.[41p]

    脐风

    　　治未病：脐在两肾之间，任冲胃三脉之所系也。儿之初生，断脐护脐，不可不慎。故断脐之时，隔衣咬断者，上也；以火燎而断之，次也；以剪断之，以火烙之，又其次也。护脐之法：脐既断矣，用软布缠裹，待干自落，勿使犯去也。三朝洗儿，当护其脐，勿使水渍入也。脐落之后，当换包裙，勿使尿湿浸及脐中也。如此调护，则无脐风之病。所谓上工治未病，十得十全也。　　治初病：儿生旬白之内，脐风为恶病也。凡觉小儿喷嚏多啼，此脐风欲发之侯，急抱儿向明晶处审视，口中上腭有泡，如珠如米成聚，此病根也。其色白者，初起也；黄者，久也。可用银穵耳轻手刮出，煎甘草薄荷汤拭洗之，预取桑白皮汁涂之。自此日日视之，有即去之，不可因循，以贻后祸。所谓中工治初病，十全六七也。[42p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[42p]

    Navel Wind

    Preventing Illness Before It Occurs: The navel is located between the two kidneys and is connected to the Ren, Chong, and Stomach channels. When a child is born, careful attention must be paid to the cutting and protection of the navel. Therefore, at the time of cutting the umbilical cord, cutting through clothing is the best method; using fire to cauterize it is the second best; cutting it with scissors and then cauterizing is the third best. As for methods to protect the navel: once the umbilical cord is cut, it should be wrapped with soft cloth and left to dry naturally, making sure not to disturb it. During the three days of bathing the infant, care should be taken to protect the navel and prevent water from getting into it. After the cord falls off, the wrapping should be changed to avoid urine from wetting the area. Proper care in this manner will prevent navel wind diseases. This is what is meant by skilled practitioners preventing illness before it occurs, achieving complete success.

    Treating Initial Illnesses: If within ten days after birth, navel wind arises, it is a serious illness. If a child displays excessive sneezing and crying, this indicates the onset of navel wind. Immediately hold the child up to a bright light and inspect the mouth: if there are blisters on the upper palate that resemble pearls or grains of rice, this is the root of the illness. If the blisters are white, they have just started; if they are yellow, they have persisted for some time. One can use a silver ear pick to gently scrape them out, then rinse with a decoction of licorice and mint and wash the affected area. It is advisable to apply the juice of mulberry bark. From then on, check daily, and if any blisters appear, remove them promptly. Do not be complacent, as it could lead to further troubles. This is what is meant by moderately skilled practitioners treating initial illnesses, achieving a success rate of around sixty to seventy percent.[42p]

    治已病：不知保护于未病之先，不知调护于初病之日，其泡子落入腹中，变为三证：一曰撮口，二曰噤风，三曰锁肚，证虽不同皆脐风也。撮口证，儿多啼，口频撮者，此脐朘痛也，可用雄黄解毒丸加乳香、投药各五分，丸如黍米大，每服五丸，竹沥生姜自然汁送下，利去恶涎良；外用蕲艾炒热杵烂，护其脐，频换，使温暖之气不绝也。不乳者不治。噤风证，牙关紧急，不能吮乳，啼声不出，发搐者不治。锁肚证，脐突青肿，肚腹胀大，育筋浮露，大便濇不通者不治。　　或问：脐风三证，古人有方，何谓不治？予曰：一腊之内，谓初生八日，草木方萌，稍有触犯，即便折伤。经曰：根于中者命曰神机，神去则机息，故噤风者乳食不得入，则机废于上矣。锁肚者便溺不得出，则机废于下矣。所谓出入废则神机化灭者是也。神出机息，虽有神丹，不可为也，岂蜈蚣、蚕、蝎诸毒药之可治耶。[43p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[43p]

    To treat existing illnesses: If one does not know to protect against diseases before they arise, nor to take care at the onset of illness, the symptoms may manifest in the abdomen as three types: the first is called "suckling mouth," the second "locked wind," and the third "locked belly." Although the symptoms differ, they all stem from "umbilical wind." The "suckling mouth" symptom is characterized by excessive crying in infants and a puckering mouth; this is due to umbilical pain. One may use a detoxification pill made of realgar, adding myrrh, with each ingredient weighing five grams. The pills should be about the size of millet, and five should be taken at a time, swallowed with natural ginger juice from bamboo to expel bad saliva effectively. Externally, use mugwort fried until warm and pounded to protect the navel, changing frequently to maintain warmth. Those who do not nurse should not be treated.

    The "locked wind" symptom involves tightly clenched jaws, inability to suckle, and silent crying; convulsions indicate that treatment should not be attempted. The "locked belly" symptom is marked by a protruding, swollen navel and an abdomen that is distended; if the bowels are blocked (hard stools), treatment is also futile.

    Someone may ask: The three symptoms of umbilical wind have prescriptions from ancient times; why can't they be treated? I reply: Within the first month, particularly the first eight days after birth, as plants and trees begin to sprout, even slight disturbances can lead to injury. The classics say: What is rooted within is called "spirit mechanism"; when the spirit departs, the mechanism ceases. Thus, in cases of locked wind, if nursing foods cannot enter, it means the mechanism at the top is rendered inactive. In locked belly cases, if excretions cannot be eliminated, it signifies the mechanism at the bottom is rendered inactive. As such, when both entry and exit fail, the spirit mechanism dissipates. When the spirit exits and the mechanism ceases, although one may possess elixirs, healing is unfeasible; can one rely on poison medicines like centipedes, silkworms, or scorpions for healing?[43p]

    变蒸

    　　变蒸非病也，乃儿生长之次第也。儿生之后。凡三十二日一变，变则发热，昏睡不乳，似病非病也。恐人不知，误疑为热而汗下之，诛罚太过，名曰大惑。或误以变蒸得于胎病者。或曰：儿之生也，初无变蒸，既生之后，当以三十二日一变，至于三百八十四日之后，又无变者何也？曰：初无变蒸者，藏诸用，阴之阖也；中有变者，显诸仁，阳之辟也；终无变者，阴阳阖辟之机成也，故不复蒸也。故儿之初生，语其皮肉则未实也，语其筋骨则未坚也，语其肠胃则谷气未充也，语其神智则未开发也，只是一块血肉耳。至于三百八十四日，然后脏腑气足，经络脉满，谷肉果菜，以渐而食，方成人也。[44p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[44p]

    Transformation and Steam

    The transformation and steam are not illnesses, but rather the stages of a child's growth. After a child is born, every thirty-two days there is a transformation; this transformation causes fever, drowsiness, and refusal to suckle, which may appear like an illness but is not. Out of fear that people may not understand, they mistakenly suspect it to be fever and excessively administer treatments, leading to what is termed "great confusion." Some might mistakenly believe that the transformation and steam arise from diseases encountered in the womb.

    Some say: when a child is born, there is initially no transformation; after birth, transformations occur every thirty-two days. Why, after three hundred eighty-four days, is there no further transformation? It is said: the absence of transformation at first signifies retention within, representing the closure of Yin; the presence of transformations signifies emergence outside, representing the opening of Yang; the absence of further transformations at the end indicates that the mechanisms of Yin and Yang have been completed, hence there is no repeated steam. Therefore, when a child is initially born, if you speak of their skin and flesh, they are not yet substantial; if you speak of their tendons and bones, they are not yet firm; if you speak of their intestines and stomach, the essence from food is not yet filled; if you speak of their spirit and intelligence, it has not yet developed; they are merely a mass of blood and flesh. It is only after three hundred eighty-four days that the internal organs are filled with energy, the channels and vessels are full, and food gradually begins to nourish them, leading to their maturation into a full human being.[44p]

    或曰：变蒸之日必以三十二日者，何也？曰：《易传》云：生生之谓易。易者变易也。不变不易，不足以见天地生物之心。人有五脏六腑，以配手足十二经络。腑属阳，以配阳卦三十二；脏属阴，以配阴卦三十二。取其一脏一腑，各以三十二日一小变，六十四日一大变。阳卦之爻一百九十二，阴卦之爻一百九十二，合岁并闰月凡三百八十四爻。所以变蒸一期之日，三百八十四，以应六十四卦爻之数也。或曰：三十二日一小变，六十四日一大变，所生者何物也？所生之物亦有说欤？曰：形既生矣，复何生也？所生者，五脏之知觉运动也。故初生三十二日一变，生足少阴癸水，肾之精也；六十四日二变，生足太阳膀胱壬水，而肾之一脏一腑成矣。此天一生水也。[45p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[45p]

    Some say: Why must the day of transformation in steaming be based on thirty-two days? It is said: The "Book of Changes" states: Life begets life, which is the meaning of change. Change refers to transformation. If there is no change, there is no transformation; without this, one cannot perceive the heart of creation in heaven and earth. Humans have five viscera and six bowels, corresponding to the twelve meridians of hands and feet. The bowels belong to yang and correspond to yang trigrams of thirty-two; the viscera belong to yin and correspond to yin trigrams of thirty-two. Taking one viscera and one bowel, each undergoes a small transformation in thirty-two days and a large transformation in sixty-four days. The yang trigram lines total one hundred ninety-two, and the yin trigram lines also total one hundred ninety-two, making a combined total of three hundred eighty-four lines for a full year including leap months. Therefore, the day of transformation in steaming amounts to three hundred eighty-four, corresponding to the number of sixty-four trigrams. Some ask: What is born from these small transformations of thirty-two days and large transformations of sixty-four days? Is there a statement regarding what is born? It is said: Once the form is born, what more is there to be born? What is born is the perceptive movement of the five viscera. Thus, initially, after thirty-two days of transformation, the foot's lesser yin water (the essence of the kidney) is generated; after a second transformation in sixty-four days, the foot's greater yang bladder water is generated, completing one viscera and one bowel of the kidneys. This is the generation of water by heaven.[45p]

    水之精为瞳子，此后始能认人矣。九十六日三变，生手少阴心丁火；一百二十八日四变，生手太阳小肠丙火，而心与小肠一脏一腑之气足矣。此地二生火也。火之精为神，此后能嬉笑也。一百六十日五变，生足厥阴肝乙木；一百九十二日六变，生足少阳胆甲木，而肝与胆一脏一腑受气足而神合矣。此天三生木也。木之精为筋，此后能坐矣。二百二十四日七变，生手太阴肺辛金；二百五十八日八变，生手阳明大肠庚金，而肺与大肠一脏一腑之气足矣。此地四生金也。金之精为声，此后始能习人语矣。二百八十八日九变，生足太阴脾己土；三百二十二日十变，生足阳明胃戊土，乃脾胃一脏一腑之气足矣。此天五生土也。[46p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[46p]

    The essence of water becomes the eye, and from this point, one can begin to recognize others. In ninety-six days there are three transformations, resulting in the hand's lesser yin heart fire. In one hundred twenty-eight days four transformations occur, leading to the hand's greater yang small intestine fire; the energies of the heart and the small intestine, as organs and viscera, become sufficient. This is where fire is generated in two ways. The essence of fire becomes the spirit, and from this point, one can begin to laugh and play. In one hundred sixty days five transformations arise, resulting in the foot's greater yin liver wood; in one hundred ninety-two days six transformations occur, resulting in the foot's lesser yang gallbladder wood; thus, the energies of the liver and gallbladder as organs and viscera become sufficient. This is where wood is generated in three ways. The essence of wood becomes the sinews, and from this point, one can begin to sit. In two hundred twenty-four days seven transformations occur, resulting in the hand's greater yin lung metal; in two hundred fifty-eight days eight transformations arise, resulting in the hand's greater yang large intestine metal, thus the energies of the lung and large intestine as organs and viscera become sufficient. This is where metal is generated in four ways. The essence of metal becomes the voice, and from this point, one can begin to learn human speech. In two hundred eighty-eight days nine transformations occur, resulting in the foot's greater yin spleen earth; in three hundred twenty-two days ten transformations arise, resulting in the foot's greater yang stomach earth; thus, the energies of the spleen and stomach as organs and viscera become sufficient. This is where earth is generated in five ways.[46p]

    土之精为肉，脾胃主四肢，此后能匍匐矣。三百五十二日十一变，生手厥阴心包络；三百八十四日十二变，坐手少阳三焦。三焦配肾，肾主骨髓，自此能坐兼能立能行矣。变蒸已足而形神俱全矣。此正如蚕之眠，不如是不足成人矣。凡一变之过，则筋骨手足以渐而坚，知觉运动以渐而发，日异而月不同。日变者，变易也；曰蒸者，发热也。袓训云：变蒸虽是胎疾，非胎热胎毒之可比，此少阴生长之气，发育万物者。儿之强者，虽有是病不觉，气弱者始见。如变后形体渐长，知识渐增，反为无病儿也，故无用治。古方黑子散，姑置之可也。其间或有未及期而发热者，或有变过热留而不除者，抑有他故，须详察之。如昏睡不乳，则不须治，待其自退。[47p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[47p]

    The essence of the earth becomes flesh, and the spleen and stomach govern the limbs; from this point, one can crawl. After three hundred fifty-two days and eleven transformations, one gives birth to hand jueyin pericardium; after three hundred eighty-four days and twelve transformations, one produces hand shaoyang sanjiao. The sanjiao is paired with the kidneys, which govern the bones and marrow; from this point, one can sit, stand, and walk. Once the process of transformation and steaming is sufficient, both form and spirit are complete. This is just like the sleep of the silkworm; if insufficient, one cannot grow into a person. Any deviation in the transformation leads to the gradual strengthening of tendons and bones, and the gradual development of perception and movement, differing by day and month. "Daily transformation" refers to change; "steaming" refers to heat. As the ancestors taught: although transformation and steaming are considered fetal ailments, they cannot be compared to fetal heat or toxicity; this is the growth energy of the lesser yin, which nurtures all things. A strong child may not feel these ailments, while a weaker child will start to show signs. As the form gradually grows and knowledge increases, it results in a healthy child; thus, there is no need for treatment. As for the ancient remedy Heizi powder, it can be set aside for now. In cases where there is premature heat or leftover heat after transformation, or other reasons, careful examination is necessary. If there is lethargy and refusal to nurse, no treatment is needed; wait for it to subside naturally.[47p]

    兼证

    　　变蒸之时，有外感风寒者，宜发散，惺惺散主之；按摩法亦可用也。内伤乳食者，宜须消导，胃苓丸主之；轻则节之可也。有被惊吓及客忤者，安神丸、至圣保命丹。如变蒸过受病，以治病为主，慎勿犯其胃气。咳嗽，甘草桔梗汤加阿胶；吐泻，理中汤加藿香叶；惊风，琥珀抱龙丸、泻青丸、导赤散。如受病后而变蒸，养正补脾为主，钱氏异功散，加对病之药。　　变蒸发热，甚者发搐，只用导赤散、泻青丸主之，效。
    　　　　　夜啼

    　　心属火恶热，心热则烦，多夜啼，或日夜啼，宜导赤散主之。
    　　　　　百日嗽

    　　小儿初生至百日内嗽者，谓之百晬内嗽。痰多者，宜玉液丸；肺虚者，阿胶散主之。此名胎嗽，最为难治。如喘嗽气促，连声不止，以致发搐，必死。[48p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[48p]

    Jian Zheng (Combining Evidence)

    At the time of transformation and steaming, individuals who are affected by external wind and cold should be treated with dispersal methods, primarily with the Xingsheng San. Massage techniques can also be applied. For internal injuries from dairy and food, it is essential to guide and digest, with Wei Ling Wan as the primary treatment; if the condition is mild, moderation is acceptable. If there is shock or disturbance, An Shen Wan and Zhi Sheng Bao Ming Dan should be used. If the illness arises from transformation and steaming, focus on treating the disease, being careful not to disturb the stomach qi. For cough, use Gan Cao Ju Geng Tang plus Ejiao; for vomiting and diarrhea, use Li Zhong Tang plus Huo Xiang Ye; for convulsions, use Hu Po Bao Long Wan, Xie Qing Wan, or Dao Chi San. If the disease arises after transformation and steaming, prioritize nurturing the upright qi and supplementing the spleen with Qian's Yi Gong San, while adding medication for the disease. In cases of transformation and steaming resulting in fever, especially severe cases with convulsions, only Dao Chi San and Xie Qing Wan should be used as the primary treatments.

    Night Crying

    The heart is associated with fire and dislikes heat; when the heart is hot, it causes agitation, leading to frequent night crying, or crying both day and night. Dao Chi San should be the primary remedy.

    One Hundred Day Cough

    For infants from birth up to one hundred days who cough, this is referred to as "One Hundred Day Cough." If phlegm is abundant, Yu Ye Wan should be administered; if there is lung deficiency, Ejiao San should be the primary treatment. This is known as "fetal cough," and it is particularly difficult to treat. If there is wheezing and difficulty in breathing, with continuous sounds leading to convulsions, it is a critical condition that may result in death.[48p]

    育婴家秘 【明　万全】

    　　　　　初生

    　　孩儿初生襁褓中，如苗秀实渐成童。四因内外能分辨，治不乖方大有功。　　幼科立方，古有定制。儿初生后病者，惟以膏丸化而服之。盖以变蒸未定，肠胃脆薄，恕不胜药，则立调治乳母之法。一岁之后，则有汤药，与大人同，但剂小耳。有因气动而病生于内者，如惊、病、虫、癖之属。惊用安神丸，内钓用木香丸，虫用安虫丸，癖用消癖丸。　　有不因气动而病生于内者，如伤乳食之属。初伤以胃苓丸和之；和之不去，以保和丸消之；消之不去，以脾积丸取之。量儿虚实，勿损胃气。　　有因气动而病生于外者，如结核、虫疥、丹瘤之属。结核用家秘内消丸，虫疥用苦参丸，丹瘤用砭法。　　有不因气动而病生于外者，如伤风、伤寒、伤暑、伤湿之属。风用泻青丸、防风惺惺散，寒用理中丸、藿香正气散，暑用凉惊丸、黄连香薷饮，湿用胃苓丸、天水五苓散。[49p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[49p]

    Nurturing Infants: A Secret Guide 【Ming Dynasty, Wan Quan】

    Newborns

    The child, just born and wrapped in swaddling, is like a sprout gradually maturing. By differentiating the four causes, we can effectively treat ailments. Establishing pediatric remedies has long been standardized. For infants sick immediately after birth, remedies must be in the form of pills dissolved in liquid. This is because their bodies are still unstable and their stomachs fragile, unable to tolerate strong medicines; thus, care should be taken in adjusting the mother's nursing methods.

    After the age of one, there are decoctions available, similar to those for adults, but in smaller doses. For diseases arising from internal disturbances, such as fright, illness, parasites, or habits, the following remedies apply: use calming pills for fright, wood incense pills for internal distress, calming parasite pills for worms, and dispelling habit pills for associated habits.

    For diseases not triggered by internal disturbances, such as those caused by improperly digested food, if the stomach is initially harmed, use stomach-purging pills; if discomfort persists, switch to regulating pills to eliminate the issue; if still unresolved, use spleen-clearing pills to address it. Be mindful of the child's vitality and do not deplete their stomach energy.

    For diseases generated externally from internal disturbances, like tuberculosis, skin parasites, or dermal tumors, employ household secret internal dissolving pills for tuberculosis, bitter herb pills for skin parasites, and acupuncture for tumors.

    For ailments not due to internal disturbances, such as colds, fevers, heatstroke, or dampness, use cooling pills and wind-relieving powder for wind; regulating center pills and aromatic herbal decoctions for cold; cooling fright pills and Huanglian Xiangru drink for heat; and stomach-purging pills and Tian Shui Wuling powder for dampness.[49p]

    胎疾

    　　胎疾初生治最难，幼科证治若空谈。丹溪妙论如绳墨，家秘书中次第看。　　小儿胎病有不必治，有不可治者。尝观《内经》颠疾之文，东垣红丝瘤之论，则儿疾之生于父母者，似乎不必治矣。一腊之脐风不治，百晬之痰咳难医，未三月而惊搐者凶，恰一月而丹瘤者死，又不可治者矣。　　今幼科有胎热、胎寒、胎肥、肥瘦、胎惊、胎黄之论，证治虽详，岂小儿常服之药也。况寒热者，胎胚之余毒也；肥怯者，父母之赋予也。非惟不可治，亦见不必治矣。惟丹溪论治胎毒者，只调治乳母，其法诚幼科之绳墨欤。　　胎寒者，母娠时多热病，乃服寒凉之药，令儿受之。生后昏昏多睡，间或吮乳泻白，此其候也。或百日之内忽病，战栗口冷，手足卷曲不伸，拘挛握拳，腹痛，昼夜啼哭不止。此生后受寒得之，亦名毒胎。宜服温补之剂，当归散主之。乳母宜服酿乳当归散。[50p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[50p]

    Fetal Illness

    Treating fetal illness at birth is the most difficult; discussing pediatric symptoms and treatments feels like empty talk. Danxi's brilliant discussions are like measuring lines; one should look through the family records systematically.

    There are some fetal diseases in children that do not necessarily require treatment, and some that cannot be treated. I have often observed the text on dizziness in the "Inner Canon" and the discussion of red silk tumors by Dongyuan, which suggest that illnesses in children resulting from parents may not need treatment. An umbilical wind after a month of pregnancy is not treated, and phlegm and cough after a hundred days are hard to cure. If a child is startled or convulses before three months, it is ominous; if a red tumor appears after one month, it may lead to death, hence it cannot be treated.

    Currently, pediatric discussions include fetal heat, cold, excess, deficiency, fright, and jaundice. Although the evidence and treatments are detailed, they are not regular medications for children. Moreover, cold and heat stem from residual toxins of the fetus; excess and deficiency come from the endowments of the parents. Not only is treatment not possible, but it also appears unnecessary. According to Danxi, to treat fetal toxins, one only adjusts the lactating mother, which truly is the guiding principle for pediatric practice.

    As for fetal cold, if the mother suffered from many heat illnesses during pregnancy and took cold medicines, the newborn receives the effects. After birth, the child may be lethargic and sleep excessively, occasionally sucking and having white diarrhea—these are signs. If, within a hundred days, the child suddenly gets sick, with shivering, a cold mouth, curled hands and feet, and continuous crying day and night, this condition may also be attributed to cold received at birth, called toxic fetus. It is appropriate to take warming and tonifying remedies, with Danggui Powder as the main prescription. The lactating mother should take fermented milk with Danggui Powder.[50p]

    胎热者，母娠时喜食煎炒辛热之物，或患热病，失于清解，使儿受之。生后目闭面赤，眼泡浮肿，常以身努，呢呢作声，或时啼叫，或时惊烦，遍身壮热，小便黄濇，此胎热也。若不早治，则丹瘤疮疖由此而生。宜用净黄连、炙甘草各等分为末，入朱砂半之和匀，生蜜调成剂，每取豆许大纳儿口中，令其咽下。乳母宜服酿乳赤芍散。　　胎惊者，母娠时曾因惊悸，气传于子，子受之，生后频频发惊，此胎癎也。不可治，治之无功。如因有热发搐者，必先啼哭，亦名胎惊，用灯心汤下东垣安神丸效。如搐不止者，此真搐也，勿治。　　小儿胎疾，有胎禀不足，并宜地黄丸。有胎毒者，如胎热法。所谓胎禀不足者，各随五脏论之：如语迟，心气不足也；心主言。行迟者，肝气不足也；肝主筋。齿发不生者，肾气不足也；发者血之余，肾主血；齿者骨之余，肾主骨。吐泻频并者，脾胃之气不足也；脾胃为水谷之府。啼声短者，肺气不足也；肺主声。[51p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[51p]

    Heat in the fetus occurs when a mother during pregnancy craves fried, spicy, and hot foods, or suffers from fever, resulting in the child inheriting this condition. After birth, the child may have closed eyes, a red face, swollen eyelids, frequently strain their body, make soft sounds, cry out at times, appear anxious, and have a hot body with yellow, turbid urine—this is fetal heat. If not treated early, it can lead to boils, sores, and abscesses. One should use equal parts of cleared goldthread and roasted licorice, ground into a powder and mixed with half as much vermilion, then combined with honey to form a paste. Administer a dose the size of a bean into the child’s mouth for them to swallow. The wet nurse should take brewed milk with red peony powder.

    Fetal fright occurs when the mother experiences fright or anxiety during pregnancy, causing this energy to transmit to the child, who then has frequent fits upon birth; this is called fetal convulsions. It should not be treated, as treatment is ineffective. If there is heat causing convulsions, it typically starts with crying and is also referred to as fetal fright. Use lantern wick decoction with Dongyuan 安神丸 for effect. If convulsions do not cease, it is genuine convulsions and should not be treated.

    Infant ailments may be due to insufficient fetal constitution, which should be treated with Rehmannia pills. If there is fetal toxicity, it should be handled as with fetal heat. The term insufficient fetal constitution refers to conditions associated with the five organs: for instance, delayed speech indicates a deficiency of heart qi; the heart governs speech. Slow movement suggests a deficiency of liver qi; the liver governs tendons. Absence of teeth or hair indicates a deficiency of kidney qi; hair is a byproduct of blood, and the kidney governs blood; teeth are a byproduct of bone, and the kidney governs bones. Frequent vomiting and diarrhea indicate insufficient spleen and stomach qi; the spleen and stomach are the reservoirs of food and water. A short cry indicates insufficient lung qi; the lung governs sound.[51p]

    脐风

    　　脐在身中号命关，冲任在此养灵根。最宜调护无伤损，才少差池减寿元。　　脐在两肾之间，谓之命门，乃人之根本也。冲任胃三脉，皆起于脐之下，任脉自中而上，至于人中，与肾脉合；冲脉二道夹任脉而上，散于舌下，与脾脉合；胃脉二道又夹冲脉、任脉而上，入于龈中，上下往来，如环无端。故男手十六岁而精行，女子十四岁而血动，任脉行、冲脉满、胃脉实也。小儿初生，三脉方具。而脐之关系尤重也。所以断脐之时，不可不慎。[52p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[52p]

    Umbilical Wind

    The navel is an essential gateway in the body, nourishing the spiritual roots through the Chong and Ren channels. It is best to maintain balance and avoid harm, as even slight discrepancies can shorten one's lifespan. The navel lies between the two kidneys and is known as the "Mingmen" or vital gate; it is the foundation of a person. The Chong and Ren channels, along with three vessels related to the stomach, all originate from below the navel. The Ren channel rises from the center, reaching up to the middle of the face and merging with the kidney channels; the two Chong channels flank the Ren channel and ascend, dispersing beneath the tongue and merging with the spleen channels. The two stomach channels also flank the Chong and Ren channels, entering the gums, circulating up and down like an endless loop. Thus, in males, semen starts flowing at age sixteen, while for females, menstruation begins at age fourteen, when the Ren and Chong channels are filled, and the stomach channels are full. When a child is born, all three channels are just beginning to form. Therefore, the relationship to the navel is particularly significant, which is why one must be careful during the cutting of the umbilical cord.[52p]

    或剪脐带太短，或结缚不紧，致外风侵于脐中；或用铁器断脐，为冷所侵；浴儿时或牵动脐带，水入生疮，客风乘虚而入，内伤于肾，肾传肝、肝传心、心传脾、脾传肺，肺蕴蓄其毒，发为脐风之病。其证面赤啼哭者，心病也；手足微搐者，肝病也；唇青口撮，痰涎壅塞者，脾病也；牙关紧者，肾病也；啼声不出者，肺病也。五脏之中略见一二脏形证者，病犹可治；悉见，不能矣。　　脐风恶候几遭伤，一腊之中最不祥。识得病在何虚起，无求无患早堤防。[53p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[53p]

    Some may cut the umbilical cord too short, or tie it too loosely, allowing external wind to invade the navel; or use metal instruments to sever the cord, exposing it to the cold; during bathing the cord may be tugged, causing water to enter and develop sores, with guest wind taking advantage of the openings, injuring the kidney. The kidney transmits to the liver, the liver to the heart, the heart to the spleen, and the spleen to the lungs, where toxins accumulate, resulting in the disease of umbilical wind. The symptoms are: a red face with crying indicates heart disease; slight twitching of the hands and feet indicates liver disease; blue lips and difficulty in swallowing with phlegm indicates spleen disease; tightness of the jaw indicates kidney disease; inability to cry indicates lung disease. If symptoms are seen in only one or two organs among the five, the condition can still be treated; if all are affected, it becomes hopeless.

    The disease of umbilical wind is a bad omen that frequently causes harm; among the months, it is the most ominous. Recognizing where the illness originates, one can guard against it early without seeking or suffering.[53p]

    小儿初生一腊之内，惟脐风为恶候也。如脐肿腹痛，啼哭不止，唇青口撮者，曰脐风；牙关紧急，吮乳不得，啼声不出者，曰噤风；肚腹紧胀，肠若雷鸣，大小便不通者，曰锁肚。此三者同一病也，但证不同耳，俗名马牙风者是也。三证多死。脐肿唇撮者，脾胃之气绝于中也；噤风乳食不入者，心肺之气绝于上也；锁肚大小便不通者，肝肾之气绝于下也。任脉止，冲脉闭，胃脉散，如之何不死？欲免此证，须要堤防。[54p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[54p]

    In the first month after a child is born, the condition known as "umbilical wind" is particularly harmful. Symptoms include a swollen navel and abdominal pain, leading to continuous crying, with blue lips and pursed mouth, which is referred to as umbilical wind; if the jaw is tightly clenched and the baby cannot suck, resulting in no crying, it is called "tight wind"; if the abdomen is tightly distended and there is rumbling in the intestines with inability to pass urine or stools, it is called "locked abdomen." These three conditions represent the same illness, but with different symptoms, commonly known as "horse tooth wind." Many children die from these three conditions. A swollen navel and pursed lips indicate that the energy of the spleen and stomach has been cut off; tight wind with inability to eat or drink indicates that the energy of the heart and lungs has been cut off; locked abdomen with no passage of urine or stool indicates that the energy of the liver and kidneys has been cut off. With the Ren channel halted, the Chong channel closed, and the stomach channel dispersed, how can one avoid death? To prevent these conditions, one must take precautions.[54p]

    预防之法：小儿初生十日之内，但见喷嚏，多啼不乳者，此将发之候也。急抱儿向明处，视其喉中悬痈，上腭有小泡如珠之样相聚者，即用银挖耳或手指甲刮去之，以软绵蘸甘草汁，拭去其血。去之早而泡白者，无虑也。其色黄或泡痕在，而落入腹中者，急用朱砂、牛黄、麝香各少许研细，取猪乳汁调稀，抹入口中；或取猪乳汁一二匙，与儿吞之佳。盖猪乳汁主小儿口噤不开最良。或用五苓散加当归、川芎、木通、木香磨汁入药内，与乳母服；或与儿服如上法。若失预防之法，其病将来而口渐撮，多啼少乳者，此腹中痛也，与内钓同。[55p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[55p]

    Prevention methods: Within ten days of a child's birth, if they frequently sneeze and cry excessively without nursing, this indicates the onset of illness. Quickly hold the child towards the light, and check their throat for any abscesses; if the upper palate has small bubbles resembling beads clustered together, use a silver instrument to scrape them away or use your fingernail. Wipe away any blood with cotton dipped in licorice juice. If you remove them early and the bubbles are white, there is no concern. If they are yellow or there are traces of bubbles that have fallen into the abdomen, urgently grind a small amount of cinnabar, ox gall, and musk into a fine powder, mix it with pig's milk to make it thin, and apply it into the child's mouth; or take a spoonful of pig's milk for the child to swallow. Pig's milk is particularly effective for infants who cannot open their mouths. Alternatively, use Wuling powder mixed with angelica, ligusticum, and muxiang, grind it into juice, and give it to the nursing mother; or administer it to the child as stated above. If the prevention methods are neglected, the illness will progress, leading to a gradually tightening mouth, increased crying, and decreased nursing, which indicates abdominal pain and is related to internal ailments.[55p]

    宜五苓散加酒当归炒、吴茱萸、木香、乳香、没药、钓藤各二分为极细末，乳拌如芡实大，纳儿口中服之无时。乳母仍服上五苓散。又取附子、桂等分为末，生姜自然汁调作饼，约一分厚，与脐相等，放在儿脐上，以纸托住，上用熨斗火熨之。如诸证悉见，不可治也。　　按古方治脐风者，初用控涎膏吐风痰，次用益胃散和胃，又用辰砂膏利惊，此良法也。又用僵蚕、全蝎、蜈蚣、蜘蛛诸毒药，以袪噤风者，此皆治其标也。不治其本而治其标，故鲜克有济者矣。[56p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[56p]

    The formula for Wuling San is to add liquor, angelica root, evodia fruit, frankincense, myrrh, and the vine of the fishing dog, each in two-tenths, ground into a very fine powder. Mix with milk to the size of aquidumbus, and give it to the child at any time. The wet nurse should continue to take the original Wuling San.

    Additionally, take aconite and cinnamon in equal parts, ground into powder, and mix with fresh ginger juice to make cakes about one-tenth thick, equal to the size of the navel, and place it on the child's navel, supported by paper. Then, heat it with an iron on fire. If all symptoms are evident, it cannot be treated.

    According to ancient methods for treating umbilical wind, first use a suppressive salve for expelling wind-phlegm, then use Yiwai San for harmonizing the stomach, and again use cinnabar salve for calming fright; this is a good method. Also, using toxic substances like silkworms, scorpions, centipedes, and spiders is to dispel the wind; all of these treat the symptoms. However, if one does not treat the root cause but only the symptoms, it is rare to achieve success.[56p]

    然父母爱子之心，必欲救之；医有活人之心，不可不救也。病轻，各如上以五苓散为主。盖五苓散是太阳膀胱之里药也。泽泻、茯苓以养心安神，官桂、吴茱萸、当归以伐肝脏寒邪之气，白朮、茯苓以去脾脏寒湿之气，木香、乳香、没药以止腹中之痛，官桂、茯苓、泽泻、猪苓又去肾中之湿，以钓藤、官桂去风之搐，以人乳和之，或乳母乳化，其气同也。若有痰者，本方加胆星末；口噤者，加白僵蚕末，更加人参末服之；如大便不通者，宜三黄解毒丸下之。此急则治其标也。去其恶毒后，如此法治之。[57p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[57p]

    Indeed, the heart of parents loving their children must desire to save them; the physician, with a heart to save lives, cannot neglect treatment. For mild illnesses, one should primarily use Wuling San. This formula is a remedy for the bladder of the taiyang. ZeXie and FuLing nourish the heart and calm the spirit; GuoGui, WuZhuYue, and DangGui expel the cold evil from the liver; BaiZhu and FuLing remove the cold dampness from the spleen; MuXiang, RuXiang, and MoYao relieve abdominal pain; GuoGui, FuLing, ZeXie, and ZhuLing also eliminate dampness from the kidneys; with Diaoteng and GuoGui, they dispel wind-induced spasms, and mixed with human milk creates a similar effect. If phlegm is present, add powdered DanXing; for locked jaws, add powdered BaiJiangCan, even further adding powdered RenShen for consumption; if there is constipation, it is advisable to take SanHuangJieDuWan for relief. This is an urgent treatment for symptomatic relief. After removing the harmful toxins, one should treat according to this method.[57p]

    脐风之病，有胎毒者，十无一生。吾见人家难于子息者，所生之子多以脐风死，如东垣所论红瘤之事，是以知之。脐疮者，其带因有所犯而落，故根未敛，溃肿而成疮也。宜白龙骨、枯矾、黄蘗三味为末，敷之甚妙。要宜常看，勿使抱裙之内有尿湿也。　　脐突者，小儿多啼所致也。脐下为气海，啼哭不止，则触动气，气动于中，脐突于外，理之常也。其状突出者光浮如吹起者，捏按则微有声。此证乡俗但遇妇人挑水来者，即抱儿以突脐当担头上触之，不拘日数，以脐入为度。每试辄效，亦良法也。[58p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[58p]

    The disease of umbilical wind, when there is fetal poison, results in nine out of ten not surviving. I have seen families struggling to have children, and those born often die from umbilical wind, as discussed regarding the case of red tumors by Dongyuan; hence I understand this. An umbilical sore occurs when the cord is disturbed and falls off prematurely, leading to incomplete healing, resulting in swelling and ulceration. It is advisable to grind together white dragon bone, dry alum, and yellow bergenia into a powder and apply it, which is very effective. It is crucial to regularly check and ensure there is no moisture from urine inside the mother's garment.

    A protruding navel commonly comes from excessive crying in infants. The area below the navel is the sea of qi; if the crying does not cease, it disturbs the qi, which moves internally, causing the navel to protrude externally—this is the natural principle. If it appears to be sticking out and looks light and buoyant, pressing it slightly produces a faint sound. As per local customs, if a woman comes to fetch water, one should hold the child and press the protruding navel against the head of the water carrier; there is no need to adhere to specific days, as the navel being pressed in is sufficient. Each time this is tried, it is effective—this is also a good method.[58p]

    吾见小儿脐突者多难养，由于多哭也。　　不乳似脐风。小儿生下三日之内，忽有不乳者，当审问之，勿以不乳似脐风治也。脐风有多啼撮唇之证，若此无之，但不乳也。有吐乳，乳之又吐者，或因拭口不净，恶物入腹也。用黄连、甘草、木香、木瓜各少许为末，每用少许纳儿口中，乳汁下。如有啼哭不乳者，此腹痛也，乃胎寒证，宜上方去黄连、甘草，加乳香、没药，以当归汤少许调药，如上喂之。如无上证，无故不乳者，问其母之乳汁多少。多者伤乳也，宜少节之，不久自思乳矣；乳少者，必有他证，心诚求之。[59p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[59p]

    I have observed that children with protruding navels are often difficult to nurture because they cry too much. If a child does not suckle, it resembles "navel wind." If within three days of birth a child suddenly refuses to suckle, one should investigate the matter and not treat it as mere "navel wind." Navel wind is characterized by excessive crying and lip-pursing, while the lack of suckling is the only symptom here. If the child vomits milk repeatedly, this may be due to an unclean mouth leading to bad substances in the stomach. Use a little of yellow calamus, licorice, angelica wood, and papaya, ground into a powder, and administer a small amount into the child's mouth to help the milk go down. If there is crying without suckling, this indicates abdominal pain, likely caused by cold in the womb; the earlier mentioned ingredients should be adjusted by removing yellow calamus and licorice, while adding frankincense and myrrh, mixing with a small quantity of Angelica soup to feed them. If there are no previous symptoms and the child refuses to suckle without reason, inquire about the mother’s milk supply. If plentiful, it may hurt the child’s ability to suckle; thus, she should reduce the amount to help stimulate the baby’s desire to feed soon. If the milk is scant, there must be another issue, and one should sincerely seek the underlying cause.[59p]

    啼哭

    　　孩儿多哭事堪怜，何事涟洳昼夜闻？饥渴痒痛如不中，拂其心意自烦棼。　　小儿初生百日一周之内，神安意静。不妄笑多哭者，易养；如日夜啼哭不止者，难养。啼与哭不同。啼者无时，有声而无泪也；哭者时作时止，大号咷而有泪也。小儿啼哭非肌则渴，非痒则痛。为父母者，心诚求之。渴者饮之，肌则哺之，痛则摩之，痒则抓之。其哭止者，中其心也。如哭不止，当以意度。盖儿初生性多执拗，凡有亲狎之人，玩弄之物，一时不在，其心不悦而哭，谓之拗哭。须急与之，勿使怒伤肝气生病也。假如又不止，请医视之。如大叫哭昼夜不止者，肝热也，宜泻青丸主之，淡竹叶汤入沙糖一豆许化下。如日夜啼哭身热烦躁者，心热也，宜导赤散加黄连，灯心汤服；或用东垣朱砂安神丸，灯心汤下，神效。[60p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[60p]

    Crying

    It is pitiable that children cry so much; what reason could they have that we hear their sounds day and night? If they are not hungry, thirsty, itchy, or in pain, their distress stems from not having their needs met. When infants are first born, for a hundred days within the first week, they are peaceful and calm. Those who do not cry much but sometimes smile are easy to care for; however, those who cry incessantly day and night are difficult. There is a difference between "crying" and "weeping." Crying happens without specific times; it is a sound without tears. Weeping, on the other hand, comes and goes at intervals, with loud wails accompanied by tears. An infant's cries indicate they are either hungry, thirsty, itchy, or in pain. For parents, it is important to sincerely seek out the cause. If the child is thirsty, give them something to drink; if they are hungry, breastfeed them; if they are in pain, soothe them; if they are itchy, scratch the spot. If the crying stops, it means their needs have been met. Should the crying not cease, one must empathize with their feelings. Infants, especially when newly born, can be quite stubborn; if someone they are close to or something they enjoy is unavailable, they may become unhappy and cry, which is termed stubborn crying. It is urgent to attend to them quickly to prevent anger from harming their liver and causing illness. If the crying continues, one should consult a doctor. If the child cries loudly without pause day and night, it indicates liver heat; in that case, it is advisable to use Qing Wan to address it, with a bit of sand sugar dissolved in bamboo leaf tea. If they are hot and irritable from constant crying day and night, this indicates heart heat; therefore, one should use Dao Chi San mixed with Huang Lian, or administer Dongyuan's Zhu Sha An Shen Wan with bamboo leaf tea for effective results.[60p]

    夜啼

    　　夜啼四证惊为一，无泪见灯心热烦。面莹颊青下脐痛，睡中频笑是邪干。　　夜啼者，脏冷也。阴盛于夜则冷动，冷动则为阴极发躁，寒甚作痛，所以夜啼而不歇也。钓藤散、益黄散主之。　　其心热烦啼者，必有脸红舌白小便赤濇之病，宜导赤散加麦冬灯心，或东垣安神丸，甚效。　　小儿夜啼，见灯即止者，此由点灯习惯，乃拗哭也。　　惊啼者，常在梦中哭而作惊，钱氏安神丸主之。　　有因客忤触犯禁忌而夜啼者，宜四圣保命丹，用灯草烧灰和药杵细，乳汁调涂乳上，今儿吮之，更以术法验之。[61p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[61p]

    Night Crying

    Night crying often surprises like a sudden shock; with no tears, I see the lamp's light, feeling heatedly troubled. My face is bright, yet my cheeks are pale, with pain below the navel. In sleep, I frequently smile; is it a result of some evil influence?

    Night crying indicates an internal coldness. The yin is strong at night, causing cold movement, and if it moves too much, the yin becomes excessive and raises disturbances, which, if severe, can lead to pain; hence the night crying does not cease. Use Diaoteng powder or Yihuang powder to treat it.

    For those whose night crying is due to a heated, troubled heart, there must be symptoms like a red face, white tongue, and dark, scant urine; it is advisable to use Daochi powder with Mai Dong and the lamp’s heart, or Dongyuan Anshen pills for significant efficacy.

    For children whose night crying stops upon seeing a lamp, this is due to the habit of lighting lamps; they are merely crying out for effect.

    Those who cry out in fright often do so in a dream, startled; Qian's Anshen pills can treat this.

    If someone cries at night due to a guest offending or violating taboos, it is advisable to use the Four Saints Life Preservation Pill. Grind the lamp’s straw ashes finely with the medicinal powder, mix with breast milk, and apply it to the breast; let them suck it today and also verify it with techniques.[61p]

    胎惊

    　　脐风发搐，此胎毒也。儿生一腊之内，多啼不乳，或撮唇或牙关紧，或肚大，或脐突发搐者，此胎惊也，谓之真搐。生于内者，气喘痰鸣，手足冷者，不治。　　变蒸发搐，此胎病也。因变蒸之后，或伤风，或伤乳，或吃惊，或发搐。百日之内，搐有真假，皆曰胎惊。真搐者，频发必死。假搐者少，宜散风化痰安神，至圣保命丹主之。百日以后，发搐口中气热，此肝旺病也，宜泻青丸，竹叶汤入沙糖少许化服，后以至圣保命丹安其神。如逢变蒸之期必发搐者，此胎癎也，自内生者。若不急洽，后成终身之病。宜安魂镇心定魄，频频细与服之，以不发为度，秘传三圣散主之。　　胎癎者，由儿在腹中受惊气得之，生来便有是病，不可治也。[62p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[62p]

    Fetal Disturbance

    When the umbilical wind causes convulsions, this indicates fetal toxicity. If a child is born within a year and cries excessively without nursing, exhibits lip pursing or has tight jaw, shows a big belly or has a protruding umbilicus leading to convulsions, this is called fetal disturbance, specifically true convulsions. If the child shows internal signs like wheezing with phlegm and has cold hands and feet, it is untreatable.

    As for convulsions due to changes in vaporization, this signifies a fetal ailment. After experiencing such changes, the child may suffer from wind-related illnesses, poor lactation, fright, or convulsions. Within the first hundred days, there are both true and false convulsions, all classified under fetal disturbance. True convulsions that recur frequently are fatal. False convulsions are less frequent and should be treated by dispersing wind, transforming phlegm, and calming the spirit, using the Supreme Life-preserving Elixir as the main remedy. After a hundred days, if convulsions occur with hot breath, this indicates a dominant liver condition, and one should use the Qing San to purge and a bamboo leaf decoction with a little sugar to disperse and serve afterwards with the Supreme Life-preserving Elixir to calm the spirit.

    If convulsions occur during a phase of vaporization, this is called fetal epilepsy, arising from internal disturbances. If not treated promptly, it may lead to lifelong conditions. It is advisable to calm the soul, stabilize the heart, and establish the spirit, administering it frequently in fine doses as a preventive measure, with the secret prescription of the Three Sacred Powders as the main remedy. Fetal epilepsy arises from the child being startled in the womb, manifesting as a condition from birth that cannot be treated.[62p]

    百晬欬

    　　小儿百日内有痰嗽者，谓之百晬嗽。或因出胎之时暴受风寒，或因浴儿之时为风所袭，或因解换褓裳，或出怀喂乳，皆风之自外入者也。或因乳脉涌出，吞咽不及而错喉者；或因啼哭未定，以乳哺之，气逆呛出者，此病之从内出者也，皆能为咳。如汗下调理之剂，难与服之。盖胃气方生，恐药伤也。故曰；小儿百晬咳难治。　　如上百晬嗽一岁及三岁者，加以痰涎壅塞，逆气冲并，以致发惊搐者，多不可治。盖小儿无知，痰在咽中，不会吞吐，往来自任故也。将作搐者，急进朱砂膏以降去之。搐止者吉，频搐者凶。故头摇者、目带上视者、闭目呻吟者、手足摆舞者，肩息胸突者、喉中痰鸣者、口噤不乳者、喘不休者、手足冷者、咬牙者，皆死也。[63p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[63p]

    On Coughing in Infants

    When an infant coughs with phlegm within a hundred days of birth, it is termed "cough of a hundred days." This may occur due to exposure to cold and wind at the time of birth, or from being affected by wind while bathing, or from changing clothes, or while being fed from the breast; all are instances of wind entering from the outside. It may also arise from excessive milk flow that the infant cannot swallow properly, causing choking; or from crying without being settled, where feeding leads to reversed airflow causing coughing — these are internal causes of the condition that can also lead to coughing.

    Medicines that induce sweating or purging are difficult to administer as the stomach Qi is just beginning to develop, and there is fear that medication could be harmful. Therefore, it is said that treating cough in infants within the first hundred days is challenging.

    If the cough persists beyond one year to three years, with phlegm and mucus causing blockage and reversed airflow leading to convulsions, treatments are often ineffective. Infants are not aware, and with phlegm in the throat, they cannot manage swallowing or expectoration, leading to distress. If convulsions are imminent, apply vermillion ointment promptly to alleviate the symptoms. If convulsions cease, it is a good sign; frequent convulsions, however, are ominous.

    It is fatal when an infant exhibits symptoms such as head shaking, upward gazing, closed eyes with groaning, restlessness of hands and feet, chest retraction, sounds from the throat indicating phlegm, refusing to breastfeed, persistent wheezing, cold extremities, or grinding teeth — all indicate impending death.[63p]

    古今图书集成医部全录卷四百八

    小儿初生诸疾门

    　　　　婴童百问 【明　鲁伯嗣】

    　　　　　初诞

    　　杨氏云：初生拭口不前，恶秽入腹，则腹满气短，不能饮乳者，宜用茯苓丸加减治之。又法：下胎毒，临产落草时，浓煎淡豉汁服极好；不可与辰砂、黄连、轻粉等。其或乳食宿滞不化，当用消乳丸化积温脾等剂治之。[64p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[64p]

    Collection of Medical Knowledge from Ancient to Modern Times, Volume 480

    Various Diseases of Newborns
    Hundred Questions on Infants
    By Lu Boshi (Ming Dynasty)

    Initial Birth

    Yang says: If the newborn's mouth is not wiped promptly, and foul substances enter the stomach, it will cause abdominal fullness and shortness of breath, preventing the baby from nursing. It is advisable to treat this with modified Poria pills. Also, for expelling fetal toxins during childbirth, taking a strong decoction of light fermented soybean juice is highly effective; avoid giving cinnabar, huanglian, or lead powder. If there are undigested remnants of milk or food, one should use digesting milk pills or remedy formulas to resolve the accumulated warmth and strengthen the spleen.[64p]

    变蒸

    　　巢氏云：小儿变蒸者，以长气血也。变者上气，蒸者体热。仲阳云：变者易也。又云变蒸者，自内而长，自下而上，又身热，故以生之日后至三十二日一变，每变毕即觉性情有异于前。何者？生长脏腑意智故也。何谓三十二日长骨添精神？人有三百六十五骨，以象天数，以应期岁，以分十二经络。故初生至三十二日，一变生癸，属足少阴经肾脏，藏精与志；至六十四日，二变一蒸生壬，属足太阳经膀胱腑，其发耳与尻伶。肾与膀胱合，俱主于水，天一生水，地六成之。至九十六日三变生丁，属手少阴经心脏，藏神，其性为喜；至一百二十八日，四变二蒸生丙、属手太阳经小肠腑，其发汗出而微惊。心与小肠合主火，地二生火，天七成之。[65p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[65p]

    Transformation and Steam
